description: The hard, minerite structures forming the body's internal framework, supporting and protecting soft tissues. Bones are the body's architecture—hidden within yet essential to every posture and movement. They endure beyond flesh, carrying memory of the body long after life departs.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'tho' /θo/, where the dental fricative breathes with ancient patience—bone outlasting the soft tissues it supports. The round 'o' suggests bone's solid form. Then 'la' extends with liquid grace, the long bones of limbs. Finally 'u' grounds deep and solid, the weight and density that gives bone its strength.
grammatical_notes: Used collectively for the skeleton or individually for specific bones. 'sheloi tholau' (many bones / the bones). May be modified for location: 'tolae tholau' (leg bone). Metaphorically used for essential structure: 'themo tholau' (the thing's bones / core structure).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Contemplation of bones features in cemetery meditations, revealing the body's impermanence. What seems solid will become dust; the framework returns to earth.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Bones carry ancestral memory—archaeologists read lives in skeletal remains. Traditional crafts used bone as tool, ornament, and oracle, honoring the structure that outlasts flesh.

tags.physical: Hard internal framework of the body
